likes: I mostly like the ease of use of QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise. There is a bit of a learning curve, but once familiar, it is an easy program to use. Being able to run reports for entire jobs was extremely helpful. You can see where expenses are going and move them if needed.
dislikes: There wasn't much of a mobile app for the Desktop Enterprise, which makes sense, but if there was some way to utilize it while in the field, it would be great. I'd like all the standard functions from the desktop version on a mobile device.
use_case: QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise helps me track, predict, and manage expenses all in one place. Running reports for entire jobs is extremely helpful to see where expenses are going and adjust them if needed. Reliable Accounting with Room for Mobile

likes: I think QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise is pretty easy to use, which is very helpful for my company, especially since we have several junior accountants. It's nice knowing that we can usually figure out a fix on our own because there's the largest knowledge base out there, filled with solutions from many users. We don't have to call our CPA for advice every time we get stuck. It's also a lot better in terms of connecting directly with our payroll and payment processors and other data inputs we need for accounting, so we don't have to manually input data entries.
dislikes: I mean, there's definitely advantages to having a cloud based offering. I think for us right now, the desktop experience outweighs the cost of having the full cloud offering. But you do have to do some more manual work in terms of backing up your data. There was a little bit of a bait and switch, I think, in terms of how QuickBooks sold us and how easy the onboarding would be and how helpful they would be. But, ultimately, we did figure it out largely on our own.
use_case: I find QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise improves connectivity with payroll and payment processors, reducing manual data entry. Streamlined Accounting with Robust

likes: QuickBooks, in general, is one of the most extensive and complete accounting software options available on the market today. What I like about QuickBooks is that it gives you the ability to handle accounting tasks without actually being an accountant. The level of customization and the wide range of features make QuickBooks an especially helpful tool for any company, and particularly for business owners.
dislikes: Because QuickBooks has such an extensive range of features, the thing I like least is the learning curve required to use it to its full potential. There are so many different aspects and tools within QuickBooks that it takes time, patience, and dedication to really learn how to navigate everything effectively.
use_case: When you’re running a business or working for a small company that doesn’t have the funds or ability to hire a full-time accountant, QuickBooks makes it possible to handle many of the tasks an accountant would normally take care of. likes: Honestly, what I love most about QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise is how it just handles complexity without making you feel it. The advanced inventory tracking is a lifesaver for businesses juggling multiple locations or warehouses, and the customizable reporting actually gives you numbers that mean something to your business — not just generic templates.
dislikes:
the pricing is probably the biggest pain point. It's a significant investment, especially for smaller businesses that don't necessarily need all the enterprise-level features — it can feel like you're paying for a lot you'll never use.
The interface also feels a bit stuck in time. It gets the job done, but compared to modern cloud-based tools, it looks and feels dated. Navigating some of the deeper features can be clunky, and the learning curve for new users is steeper than it should be in 2025.
use_case: The biggest problem it solves for us is keeping everything in one place. Before, we were juggling spreadsheets, separate inventory tools, and manual reporting — it was messy and way too easy to make errors. Enterprise pulls all of that together, which alone has saved us a ton of time and headaches. Handles Complexity with Powerful Inventory Tracking and

likes: What I like most about QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise is how straightforward and user-friendly it feels. We’re able to handle invoicing, reporting, and everyday accounting efficiently, without adding unnecessary complexity.

likes: I love the inventory portion and the invoicing features of QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise. I've only been using it for about three years, but I find it easy, simple, and very efficient. So it's pretty efficient.
dislikes: Sometimes I have issues or difficulties with things that were in the past year and need fixing this year. It might be that I just need to learn more, but that seems to be my biggest issue. Also, the initial setup was quite challenging when transitioning from the old system to QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise, especially transferring over a full year and getting it to balance. It would have been nice if I could have linked the two systems for a smoother transfer.

QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise stands out as a powerful, reliable accounting solution, especially for growing business that need more capability than standard QuickBooks versions.
Although enterprise has a rich feature set, the interface remains intuitive for users familiar with QuickBooks. Tasks like invoicing, reporting and inventory tracking are straightforward, with clear menus and logical workflows.
Implementation is generally smooth, especially for teams that already use QuickBooks products. Data migration tools and setup wizards help onboard financials, customers and inventory without being overly technical.

QuickBooks desktop enterprise tends to be significantly more expensive than other QuickBooks products and many cloud alternatives.

likes: I like QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise for its enterprise-grade, US retail/wholesale tailored inventory accounting, which is its biggest strength. The advanced inventory module is the single most valuable feature for my inventory-heavy business, and it’s the primary reason I chose it over QuickBooks Online or generic accounting software. It’s explicitly built for US multi-location, multi-channel retail/wholesale workflows and handles the scale of over 100k SKUs and millions of annual inventory transactions. It remains fully compliant with US GAAP and IRS inventory valuation rules. I also find the initial setup to be structured and robust, tailored for US mid-to-enterprise inventory-heavy businesses.
dislikes: The native integration with my core e-commerce platform (CommerceV3) has sync delays (up to 24 hours) for US online sales/inventory data—critical during peak US sales seasons; integrating QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise with Tableau requires a custom API build (expensive and time-consuming) with no pre-built connector; and there’s no native integration with US email marketing tools (e.g., Klaviyo) to sync customer purchase/inventory data for targeted US regional campaigns (e.g., a Florida “out-of-stock” alert for a popular SKU). Third-party middleware also adds cost and latency—Zapier Premium for QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise integrations costs an extra $200+/month, and syncs still have 1–2 hour delays.
use_case: I use QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise to eliminate inaccurate, siloed inventory accounting, and solve multi-state sales tax compliance for my US retail and warehouse operations. Tailored for US Enterprise

likes:
QuickBooks Enterprise is a powerful accounting software designed for growing businesses that need more advanced tools than QuickBooks Online or QuickBooks Desktop Pro. It offers strong features like customizable reporting, advanced inventory tracking, user permissions, and the ability to handle large amounts of data without slowing down.

What I like most is how scalable it is—you can add users as your business grows, and it allows different levels of access, so team members only see what they need to. The reporting features are also very detailed and can be customized to fit unique business needs.

likes: We’ve been using QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise for a while now, and overall, it’s been a solid tool for managing our finances. The reporting features are detailed, and having everything in one place really helps with staying organized. There was a bit of a learning curve at first, especially with some of the more advanced features, but once we got the hang of it, it made a noticeable difference in how efficiently we handle accounting tasks. It’s definitely built for businesses that need more power and flexibility than the basic versions offer.
dislikes: We tried using QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise, but it ended up being more frustrating than helpful for our team. The interface feels outdated, and navigating through some of the features isn't very intuitive. We also ran into issues with multi-user access—things would lag or freeze when more than one person was working in the system. For how much it costs, we expected a smoother experience. It might work well for some businesses, but for us, it just didn’t fit.
use_case: QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise helps solve several problems we used to struggle with—mainly around managing large volumes of data, handling complex inventory, and getting detailed financial reports. Before switching, we had to juggle multiple systems and spreadsheets, which was time-consuming and error-prone. Now, everything from payroll to job costing is in one place, which saves us time and reduces mistakes. The customizable reporting is especially helpful for making better business decisions, and having strong user permissions adds a layer of security we didn’t have before.

bonus_data.title: It has streamlined our accounting processes with powerful, easy-to-use tools

likes:
As a Finance Executive handling diverse responsibilities—such as accounts receivable, payables, reconciliations, and reporting—QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise has consistently stood out as a powerful and dependable accounting solution.

What I appreciate most is its depth and control in managing financial data. The advanced reporting capabilities allow me to generate customized, accurate financial statements that meet both internal management needs and external audit requirements. Features like batch invoicing, customizable templates, and multi-user permissions make it ideal for a finance team setting, allowing secure collaboration and efficient division of tasks.

The software’s strong audit trail functionality ensures transparency, which is crucial during internal audits or when preparing for external financial reviews. I also find the inventory management tools very effective, especially for clients operating with large product lines.

Unlike some cloud-based platforms that prioritize simplicity, QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise provides granular control over every financial aspect of the business. This makes it especially suitable for mid-sized organizations that require a robust accounting infrastructure without moving to full-scale ERP systems.
dislikes:
While QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise offers impressive accounting capabilities, there are a few areas that could be improved, especially in today’s increasingly remote and cloud-based work environments.

The most significant limitation is that it's a desktop-based platform, which means you are tied to a physical location or network setup unless you invest in third-party hosting solutions. For finance professionals or teams that work remotely or across multiple locations, this can create accessibility challenges. In contrast, cloud-based alternatives offer more flexibility for real-time collaboration and access from anywhere.

The user interface, although functional, feels outdated and less intuitive compared to more modern platforms. For new users, the learning curve can be steep, especially if they are unfamiliar with the structure of enterprise-level accounting systems. Some features are also buried in menus, making them harder to discover without prior training.

Additionally, the licensing and upgrade costs can be a barrier for smaller businesses. Annual renewals and add-on modules (e.g., payroll, inventory) can increase the total cost of ownership over time.

In summary, while the software is reliable and feature-rich, its lack of cloud integration, outdated design, and cost structure may not be suitable for every team, especially smaller or fully remote organizations.
use_case:
QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise is solving several critical challenges related to financial management, data accuracy, and process efficiency. It helps me streamline complex accounting tasks such as managing multiple clients’ accounts receivable and payable, reconciling intercompany transactions, and tracking payments and invoices in one centralized system. This reduces manual errors and saves significant time in data entry and reconciliation.

The software’s robust reporting features allow me to generate detailed financial statements and aging reports quickly, which improves decision-making and cash flow management. Additionally, QuickBooks Enterprise’s ability to handle large data volumes and integrate with other financial tools benefits me by ensuring scalability and smoother workflow as the business grows. Trusted Accounting Software for

likes: QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise is a powerful accounting solution tailored for growing businesses. It offers advanced inventory management, robust reporting tools, and multi-user access, making it ideal for companies that need more control over their financial data. The software handles complex accounting tasks efficiently, though it may require some training for new users. Overall, it's a reliable and scalable solution for businesses seeking detailed financial oversight.
dislikes: While QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise is feature-rich and powerful, it can feel a bit overwhelming at first—especially for those new to accounting software. The interface isn't as modern as some cloud-based alternatives, and setup can take some time. However, once you get the hang of it, the level of control, customization, and depth it offers is absolutely worth it. The learning curve turns into a strength because you’re working with a tool that truly grows with your business.
use_case: QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise helps solve several key problems in managing business finances—especially around accuracy, efficiency, and scalability. It streamlines tasks like inventory tracking, payroll, advanced reporting, and managing multiple users or locations. For me, it saves time by automating complex processes and reduces errors with its powerful accounting tools. The ability to generate customized reports also makes it easier to make informed business decisions. Overall, it’s helped keep financial operations organized and scalable as the business grows.

likes: I can honestly say that the only thing I like about this software is that I already am familiar with it. I know it well and use it for 3 different companies.
dislikes: I have the contractor edition of QB Enterprise. It is lacking in many ways. The most important feature I see it missing is the ability to account for retention billings. Although I have a work around, there is absolutely no sure-fire way to track retention with this software. Years ago, I had an employee doing my billing. She FORGOT to bill retention on several projects, costing me a lot of money. I have pointed this out to the QB staff and submitted requests over the past 10 years, only to be told that QB takes its suggestions from clients very seriously. Well, I can tell you that is not true. If you look through the comments on the QB site, many contractors are asking for the same thing. After 10 years of asking I've given up.

likes: QB Desktop is good at being a catch all for whatever daily simple accounting needs your company may have, and many complicated ones as well. dislikes: There are some glitches like when creating a journal entry, if the first line has no account in it, the journal entry will disappear, and if you filter out all classes besides one in a balance sheet it will throw AR and AP out of wack. I also wish there was an option to generate a P&L w comparison of current period, to previous, YTD, AND budget. Currently you only get to compare either to previous period and/or YTD, OR budget.

likes: Quickbooks Desktop allowed us as a small company at the time to use the software for our accounting needs without having a huge software expense. It was also very easy to use and people could learn to use it quickly.

use_case: We needed accounting software and Quickbooks was available at a good price. Worked Best When Our Company

likes: You can create invoices. That is about as far as I would go with using quickbooks enterprise.
dislikes:
Quickbooks enterprise promises many features, but anything beyond invoicing is highly laborious and rarely works. Do not rely on Quickbooks for anything beyond normal accounting.
Basic tasks are made difficult by horrible UI decisions with little to no control over customization. We are continuously shocked by the decisions their devs and product managers make with usability and functionality.

Something as simple as creating or editing invoice templates is dreadful and riddled with bugs. Editing price lists feels like some sort of sick joke.
Sales order fulfillment is about 10% of the way to something useful.

Multi-user fails consistently. Most issues must be fixed in single-user mode bringing everyone to a halt. 

Integrations out of the box are almost non-existent, mainly due to Quickbook's ancient and unmaintained architecture.

On top of everything wrong with the software, support is useless and often worsens matters. Always be prepared to run through the same 2 hour checklist, including uninstalling and reinstalling before support will even answer basic questions. I have yet to have support assist in fixing an issue correctly. I have resolved what I could on my own through lengthy research. Some problems have been unresolvable, and QuickBooks shows no interest in assisting. These are significant software-breaking issues, like Quickbooks failing to generate a pairing code for a scanner device, rendering WMS entirely unusable.
use_case: Creating invoices. This is not benefiting us because every other step of the process is detrimental to our operations.

bonus_data.title: Everything is tedious or doesn't work at all

likes: QB is a great product requiring minimal implementation costs and IT expertise. It's great for a startup or a small business that does not have a lot of resources for more robust and sophisticated systems.
dislikes: In my opinion, it lacks controls and segregation of duties. The product's simplicity lends itself to a very short learning curve, but with that comes a product with many cookie-cutter processes and workflows.
use_case: Our accounting software is utilized to record all transactions and generate financials. Our most extensive functionality is accounts payable, bank reconciliation and recording journal entries that summarize the revenue data from our POS. Using classes to segregate our studios and generate separate entity financials is helpful for our reporting.

likes: The integration with SA is simple and easy to learn. It takes a little getting used to from other integrations we have had with QBs, but I like it more and more as time goes on.
dislikes: The only "downside" I have seen so far is that you can only attach one account with SA. Our firm has multiple accounts with the same bank, and it would be helpful if we could integrate 2 of them. Some other things could be more accessible and better for processing checks. But overall, we are happy with our decision.
use_case: Keeping everything more organized between the two programs. Making the reporting more accessible in each program has greatly benefited the bookkeeper and the managing partners.

likes: Quickbooks makes creating invoices and sending them to the appropriate clientele easy. It is straightforward to navigate and able to use from pretty much anywhere
dislikes: The updates for Quickbooks. I dread them. Every time Quickbooks sends out a new update, it's like they are trying to make the system harder for people to use. It is frustrating to get used to doing things for so long and knowing where everything is located, and then you get hit with an update that changes everything around and makes it much more difficult to use. Also, anytime something is not working correctly on the site; I cannot receive any help unless I pay more. That is ridiculous. I already paid for Quickbooks; then, I am expected to upgrade and pay extra if their site is not working correctly. I had called support before when the site was having a bunch of glitches and problems and was told on the phone that they could not help me unless I upgraded my package/membership with them.
use_case: Unfortunately, I have more bad things to say about Quickbooks than good. I have many issues that I cannot figure out a solution to on the site, and they aren't fixing the problems, so I am not being benefited with any answers.

likes: Quickbooks can perform numerous tasks, making its versatility exceptional. We utilize various features, including inventory management, payroll, and invoicing services. Overall, it is a reliable software that can accomplish much more than people realize, such as job costing and inventory tracking.
dislikes: For larger businesses, Quickbooks may not be the ideal choice, as it may not be capable of accommodating their needs. As a company expands and reaches a 10 million dollar revenue mark, Quickbooks may no longer be sufficient. Additionally, creating custom reports may be challenging for those who are not proficient in strings and commands.
use_case: Quickbooks offers a comprehensive solution for managing payroll, benefits, taxes, and accounting. It also aids in sending reminders for past-due invoices, which is particularly beneficial in our industry, where customers often have varying net terms. It can be challenging to keep track of all these aspects, and Quickbooks simplifies the process.

likes: I love that it integrates with so many things and quickly absorbs a lot of data into readable chunks. I can run my day-to-day details and large-scope vision of my business.
dislikes: It's sometimes very clunky to understand and the wide variety of features can quickly get confusing. Visually it lacks modernization and can be challenging to learn.
use_case: Keeping expenses and profits straight, formats reports for tax season, and Vendor management is simple and straightforward. This helps direct all flows of business finances into one place.

bonus_data.title: Helpful with lots of valuable features but sometimes superfluous visually

likes: Many things that Quickbooks can do. Versatility is outstanding. We use many services such as inventory, payroll, invoicing. All around it is a solid software and can do more than most think. Such as inventory and job costing.
dislikes: Its not great for bigger businesses. when you grow to a 10 mil company you tend to out grow it. Some of the custom reporting can be a bit tricky to customize if you are not familiar with strings and commands.
use_case: Payroll, benifits, payroll, taxes, accounting. It helps with sending out reminder for past due invoices as well. That is much needed in our industry as many customers have different net tems. It's a lot to keep up with.

likes: Quickbooks provides not only the basic fundamental items needed for any business to maintain their finances, but it also provides in-depth reporting and analysis so that you can plan/prepare and avoid pitfalls.
dislikes: Integration with other software isn't always seamless. Because Quickbooks is the premier software of choice for financing, it integrates with nearly any ERP software. However, that doesn't mean the integration is always perfect.

bonus_data.title: Great for accounting and reporting, not so much warehouse and logistics

likes: QuickBooks is really intuitive and easy to use, it's very simple to teach new employees to use the program even if they were not familiar with it before. Mostly point and click with all categories organized in appropriate areas. Offers custimization options for accounts, reports, templates, etc. Does offer add-ons/apps and various accounting methods based on company needs.
dislikes: It's great for small businesses but not so great for manufacturing with multi-level BOM's. We have been able to find some way around things, but it would be great if there was more depth for sub-assemblies, finished assemblies and raw materials. With multi-level BOM's builds become a hassle, so properly relieving inventory is a tedious and very involved process. While QuickBooks offers addon apps some integrations have flaws that Quickbooks themselves could easily solve for even better integration. Lastly, some reporting features are limited. With the amount of data available, even Advanced Reporting is not able to generate certain reports. Raw data access is possible, which is great but it does require knowledge of the database structure. Overall, it's a functional software, but could be easily outgrown by mid-sized businesses.
use_case: QuickBooks is constantly adding updates and new features. Hopefully, in the future, they will have much more available functionality that could solve additional problems mid-size businesses have. Good for Small Business but not for

likes: The Quickbooks Desktop Enterprise software has excellent user support. Great and integrated features. User-friendly software, very easy to use, and no need for training or prior language. Flexible for business daily activities and project management. bonus_data.title: Enterprise is the all in one business must have

likes: Quickbooks is a good program for tracking finances, pricing, and payroll. It works well for us the mass majority of the time. We don't have many issues with it. The issues we do have can usually be resolved quickly. Their support team is great, quick, and efficient.
dislikes: Quickbooks can be finicky. The system they use does not like having locked-down folders or rights. Every so often we will have problems with Multi-user mode or with the services running properly. Usually, a reboot of the services or the server will fix the issue. It doesn't happen too often so it's not a major issue for us.
use_case: Quickbooks lets us track what bills we have, what has been paid, when they were paid, and the check numbers used for paying them. It is also used for paying our employees every two weeks. It lets us import hours from our time tracking software, TimeClock Plus without issues. The import is quick and easy. Quickbooks

likes: That it was easy to cancel and back up, and remove users no longer needing it.
dislikes:
There were many issues with it, starting with the downloads from the bank. You would have a full month reconciled, a month with about 1000 transactions in it. Then magically after doing that it would just unreconciled an item that was in that reconciliation. This meant having to go back and do the full reconciliation again. In some cases back multiple months and re-reconcile up to 3 months again after doing it, then maybe repeat that adventure again in the same month.

Then it would download duplicates of transactions, not letting you know it was a potential duplicate. The search functionality to try to find things absolutely was atrocious.

If you owed staff money for reimbursement, or to withhold funds from their cheque, you could not have a staff record also be showing as an account payable or account receivable, so you'd have to create a second record for them as one of the others, yet you couldn't use the same name as it said it was a duplicate.

I tried to call the help desk, but you needed a subscription to "ask for help" to talk to a live person, so left to try to scour the forums for answers.

Trying to migrate it to Quickbooks Online was done with a wing of hope and a prayer and took me 20 hours on the phone with 9 people in 4 different departments to actually get it done. Every person I talked to told me different things.

On top of this, you pay for the privilege - at a whopping $300 a month vs the $36 a month for the same amount of users for their online version.

The Payroll portion of Quickbook Enterprise - well you have to use Telpay as it was their supplier. Telpay at the time (and 2 years ago still didn't) have any way to actually reconcile the money that you sent them with what is paid out - unless you want to pay them $50 an hour to see if they can actually reconcile the records if you're out.

Then we had to also use a remote server to have it set up as we needed, which was yet another subscription service and when you generated your T4's they landed in some mystery folder that you had to call them to try to find and they would have to actually move your T-4's for you, then you would have to do your own upgrades, but in order to do that you had to ask for more space as there was enough space for QuickBooks, but not enough space for you to do the upgrade until the old one was removed.

While I do understand we have a higher volume of transactions than the average Quickbooks users, the issues we encountered were beyond what could be reasonably expected.. 

The cheque printing could never get it quite right,

I could go on.. but that is more than enough
use_case: They did not solve many problems, they allowed us to generate reports when I could confirm transactions were accurate. We tried sage, but it was just as bad if not worse. Using QB DeskTop

likes: There is honestly Nothing we like about QuickBooks!! We do not like any of the features that QuickBooks has because their customer support is a complete joke, and you cannot get the help you need when you need it!
dislikes: QuickBooks has destroyed our accounting by trying to fix issues that their program created. We have had to go back in and recreate months of lost customer lists etc... We had Sage/PeachTree for 25-plus years and never had the problems that we are having with QuickBooks!! When you call customer support and are put on hold for very long periods and get cut-off multiple times, you may get to speak to nit-whits that know Nothing about the actual program and all they can do is read their stock answers out of their little book of answers. You cannot talk to anyone that will take responsibility for their product or that knows anything. Anyone that has any clout with QuickBooks is so well guarded that you can never speak to them. If you are considering QuickBooks Run don't walk to another accounting software and don't make the same mistake we did and buy their pathetic program! Wish we had never switched from Sage to QuickBooks now!! We had been told by everyone for years that we needed to switch to QuickBooks because everyone knows the program and it is more user-friendly. That may have been the case years ago but in 2022 it is simply Not True.

likes: It is very user friendly. Intuitive menu's. You can create many different customized reports. Import / Export feature for reports and data makes it extremely useful. I can easily import spreadsheets of data for invoicing, sales receipts easily into Quickbooks. Exporting financial reports to Excel is very simple where you can then add change or format the spreadsheet to your liking. I love the Company snapshot feature which is essentially a dashboard that you can customize to your liking. I also process payroll through my QB Enterprise and after using many different payroll systems like ADP and Paychex, I have never had any easier than this to process a payroll, pay and file payroll taxes and quarterly filings with just a few clicks. All the journal entries are entered and it makes reconciling a breeze. What used to be a cumbersome task of of processing 3rd party payroll and then having to do all the journal entries is now a simple task with all the data built in. If I need payroll reports for a specific employee or in general, I have it at my fingertips. Processing checks and sending my vendors an electronic bill payment stub makes for a very efficient process and keeps my vendors happy knowing that their payment has been processed and exactly what is being paid. Having the class feature allows me to run highly customized reports for specific customers and then having it further broken down by job is invaluable. You can get all these features in an ERP system that costs tens of thousands of dollars to purchase and expensive annual fees to renew.
dislikes: One of my biggest dislikes or concerns is that data can be easily changed, causing problems such as reconciling differences hard to find. There is no log to track back what might have changed.
recommendations: Many problems. I have reporting at my fingertips to share with my team. My process for invoicing using the import feature from a spreadsheet saves me many hours of time for work that was previously manually entered. Likewise for sales tax, I am able to import my data from the back end of my website, and drop that data into QB Enterprise, and then run my report Sales Tax Liablility, which makes filing my sales tax reports in various states a very simple task. Quickbooks Enterprise - Stay away from expensive ERP
use_case: I think QB sometimes gets a bad wrap in that it is not as sophisticated as some ERP systems. I say totally wrong! QB can do what you need to run your business efficiently.

likes: Quickbooks is an industry-standard solution for small to medium-sized businesses. I have found that it has features that some of the big hitters for larger firms do not have (like auto-categorizing downloaded transactions and easy-to-use interfaces for vendor and customer centers, even inventory and time tracking). They also now have an interface with CRM tools. And the pricing is well suited for smaller businesses given the robust capabilities of this program. It gives you the ability to have up to 30 simultaneous users. Depending on the level of Enterprise you use, you can also work simultaneously between multiple company entities.
dislikes: I am a long-time Quickbooks user, and there is not much that I don't like about it. I would say that it is not an ideal solution for large companies. And as with most financial software solutions, you need someone with experience to set it up and use it. If you're a very small or new business, start with the Pro version. But having someone experienced with the software (or learning it yourself) will really unlock all of the best functionality.
recommendations: I help other businesses set up or move over to various Quickbooks products. I have seen many entities benefit from this program. Especially with the excellent job costing, reporting, inventory tracking, and simple accounts payable and accounts receivable workflows.
use_case: Quickbooks Desktop Enterprise is a very robust system. If you are a smaller business and new to Quickbooks, I suggest you look at their more basic products first (such as Quickbooks Pro), or hire someone experienced with the software. If you are a small to a medium-sized business needing lots of functionality, this is the best product out there.

likes: It gets the job done and keeps everything organized, for the most part. And because they NEVER changed their style, the functionality is always consistent which is nice. Sometimes it is annoying to adjust to new softwares, so this particular function can be good for some people.
dislikes: It can be extremely irritating to use at times. For example, the "search" function doesn't work half the time... I can never use it to find a transaction I am looking for, so I have to do it manually, which defeats the purpose of the search function. And then sometimes I'll open Quickbooks and it can no longer "find the file," so I have to go back through backups and find the right one, which sometimes doesn't work and takes forever to reload... and there is absolutely no reason for this. Lastly, if you make one error in reconciliation it makes it extremely difficult to rectify it. It should be way simpler to fix, but sometimes I have to unreconcile multiple months just to get back to where I need to be, and then start completely over.
recommendations: It allows me to keep my organization's finances under wraps, which, when it functions properly, works great. The P&L statements are organized and complete (though they could be a little user friendlier to navigate... for example, if you scroll too fast, it scrolls all the way to the bottom and you lose complete control, so you are forced to scroll painfully slow), past payments made by check are pretty easy to find, and it is easy to bill and pay people.
use_case: Unless you prefer old fashioned software, I would go for Quickbooks Intuit (the online version). It is better for so many reasons. You can keep all info in the cloud, you can easily share data with people, you can access your data from anywhere, and it's way more modern and easy to use. Either Quickbooks Desktop needs to update its software, or make some other serious changes to keep up.

likes: Quickbooks allows my colleagues and I to centrally track financial information. We can record notes for transactions that everyone has access to, and clearly labeled sections facilitate discussion about transactions outside of the system. Information in the system is backed up nightly to ensure data is saved. We can also create new records for each fiscal year without losing access to previous records. Cross-referencing within the system is helpful for historical purposes. This program is easy to use on Windows and integrates well with Excel.
dislikes: Software works slower from home versus in the office. This makes it difficult to record transactions in real time and can impact the experience of other users. Server connectivity may have an impact on the functionality of the program. It also uses a significant amount of power/CPU when in use. Can have an impact on battery life and other applications. Though old data is saved it is somewhat difficult to access. You must log out of the current fiscal year to view another year. The setup of the program does not exactly align with our business needs so there are some unused fields that take up space and can be visually distracting.
recommendations: Centralized hub for tracking revenues and expenses. Multiple users can make edits and record transactions. The system is flexible to allow for expense and revenue information from various systems to be imported. Also allows ease of exporting reports to Excel to share with external colleagues Quickbooks

likes: Quickbooks Desktop Enterprise works great for a single-entity company wanting advanced financial features and ease of use. It is very user-friendly, and the cost is commensurate to what you get. You can track and handle many items with QB Desktop Enterprise, including inventory, sales estimates, purchase orders, etc. Reporting is customizable and you can find or create the reports that you need. You can also attach receipts to your expenses, which is great for auditing purposes.
dislikes: Quickbooks products, including Desktop Enterprise, are not suited for multi-entity companies. It is still the same has having many separate entities and company files, and the only thing it will do is pull reports from many selected companies together, but this does not help with inter-entity transactions which need to be doubled-entered. Also, after many years, we began to have issues with our file degrading, and more and more our balance sheet would be out of balance, requiring a rebuild. To be fair, we had a really large file at the end.
use_case: Quickbooks worked great for financial tracking, for invoicing, with its own payroll solutions, and for tax reporting. The multi-user functionality helped us with the need for many people to be working in it at one time. QB Desktop

likes: It's a great application to make invoices and send it off without having to create your own invoice spread, and emailing it to clients. You can just directly email it, without really needing to write a message. It also has the option to set a due date for them to pay, and you don't have to necessarily have to do the reminding or follow up.
dislikes:
A few things I think QuickBooks can upgrade to, is have a feature where it can track how much of each product is being sold. For instance, we have a regular client that we send invoices go once a week, at the end of the week. They want to have the number of orders made per day, specific to each product. But QuickBooks can't track the one week organized into separate days. It's not set up for that. Therefore, I must create a separate excel sheet to track how many of the products they ordered, every day. It would be absolutely great if QuickBooks had the option to have a one week made invoice or an order sheet so I can put down the number of items ordered specifically to each day. 

Another suggestion is to have QuickBooks have the feature where it can automatically calculate the number of products being sold. This way, you can look back and see which products are popular and which aren't.

I'm also not a huge fan of their invoice numbering system. I like to keep the numbers sequenced, but I see that it skips numbers or I automatically get a random number.
recommendations:
The problems I had with QuickBooks, include the invoice numbering which doesn't always assign an invoice in a sequenced order. So I end manually inputting my own invoice numbers I created.

The benefits is I can easily access all my invoices on QuickBooks with a click of a button. Just swiftly type in the client name and I can see whether they paid or not, when it was made, etc. 

If I don't have to send any other messages or files, I can easily email the clients their invoice through QuickBooks.
use_case: QuickBooks is a great tool to have when you're in the office or on the go, meeting with clients. It sends off invoices easily without having to personally ask for payment or follow up on a payment. I would still use QuickBooks with another application however. This is because I think QuickBooks is missing a few features that could benefit businesses. For instance, they should have the feature where it can automatically calculate the number of products being sold. This way, you can look back and see which products are popular and which aren't, and this can really help businesses help customers with what they want. This feature can also help businesses know the forecast of the sales for next coming years.

likes: Quickbooks Desktop Enterprise is easy to use and user friendly. If you make a mistake, it's easily reversible. If you are ever stuck or confused on how to do something, there are multiple tools and resources to use to help without having to call the customer service line. I've used Quickbooks for 5+ years and love it.
dislikes: The only thing I can complain about is that the search or find bar sometimes does not work. Other than that, I have had no issues using Quickbooks.
recommendations: We use Quickbooks for everything at work. I am accounts receivable and it's super easy to enter checks, create invoices from PO's or sales orders and to connect a paper trail that I can easily find. I love going to a bill I've entered and hitting "transaction history" and it pulls up the check number I used to pay it. It's very user friendly. We run reports on everything, ex: how many times have we sold this item in the last year. Do a quick report on that item and all the information you need is right there. How many times you sold it, ordered it, and how many are pending on jobs.
use_case: It is very user friendly and easy to learn. I had no experience with any type of software like Quickbooks and I caught on very quickly. You can do a multitude of things with the software. I love that you can run reports, that's very helpful with my line of work and keeping track of inventory.

likes: I like the multi user options best. I am able to work and see what my colleagues are doing the minute they do it. If for some reason I need to work without any one else doing anything, I can do that too. The reports are also very useful. So is the find options. The more information you can give to the system the more you can get.
dislikes: Several glitchy issues. I randomly get kicked off of mine. I often get unrecoverable errors. I have spent countless hours on the phone with their customer support and they cant fix it.
recommendations: I do about 90% of my work on Quickbooks. Invoicing customers, paying bills, running reports, importing payroll. It really is quite versatile. I love that I can be a specific and detailed and then I am able to find that information when I need it.
use_case: Make sure that you set up your file from the very beginning exactly as you want it. Think things through carefully, and dont just jump on in and get it going without lots of thought. I wish we were able to completely redo our set up, it's just not feasible. Take the time to get it right from the beginning.

switched_from.reason: This was before my time, so I am not 100% sure. I think it was another church accounting software. We needed a system that integrated well with the Quickbooks POS system. They made the switch at that time.

likes: I love how Quickbooks makes it so easy organize everything. I can easily find all my customers information,balance, last order placed. I can easily duplicate an order which many customers request. I just have to make a copy of the original order.
dislikes: Sometimes, it's a little hard to look up special pricing for certain customers. I often have to go through many invoices to look up a particular style number just to see how much I charged them in the past.
use_case: I'm solving a lot of past due balance issues. Quickbooks makes it easy to just look through a list of customers who have an open balance and then I can easily email them a copy of their past due invoice.

likes: Best aspect about Quickbooks Enteprise is that it's a jack of all trades. Gives you decent functionality into several functions that would cost a lot more with a traditional ERP. The advanced inventory module really helps track bin locations for multiple products thus improving the pick/pack efficiency. QB has also increasingly added more and more integrations in areas such as CRM , Barcoding, and Payroll.
dislikes: Least is the speed on which it runs. Our business has over 1000 SKUs and generating reports or even making certain inventory adjustments cause our RDP to slow down far too frequently. Quickbooks definitely slows down when hosted on a server which at times slows down the day-to-day workflow.
recommendations: We are keeping great track of our inventory within our warehouse. We've been able to create "locations" for all of our pallet racks and shelves and then we are able to assign items to multiple locations. For example, pickable shelf of SKU "Dress331" and where the cases of that item are stored. This has greatly increased our pick/pack efficiency and given us a better understanding of inventory as a whole on our balance sheet.
use_case: While it lacks in convenience of access to QBO, QBE provides far more functionality under one roof. For Quickbooks Online, you would have to find an advanced inventory module to integrate with which increases the number of windows you'll have to work out of. Quickbooks Enterprise keeps everything under one roof. The next step up to keep everything under one roof with a ERP can be very costly and that jump should only be made when Quickbooks processing speed can't keep with your number of transactions.

likes: QB is great for keeping track of customers, invoices, payments, vendors, etc. It organizes all of the basic information you need to keep track of to maintain a business.
dislikes: The most frustrating thing about QB would have to be how slow it is. It freezes up and has locked me out from time to time. But, that's not a total deal breaker due to how helpful it is.
use_case: Years prior to using QB, our organization wasn't the best. It has helped us reference things quickly & efficiently.

likes: I love QBE for its robustness. It does everything an accountant could possibly want in an accounting package. It's easy to use; it's extremely accurate; it's safe; it has a reasonable amount of safety features built-in, but not excessive. QBE is intuitive in that it has built-in reminders of tasks the user and the accountant need to perform.
dislikes: Intuit does have an online accounting system called QBO, but it does not hold a candle to QBE. I wish the full-blown QBE could be setup as the online version.
recommendations: I install QBE in small to medium size businesses SMD's to handle all of their accounting needs. As the Accountant I am able to monitor the financial pulse of the business and make recommendations to the business owners/operators as to adjustments/changes they might want to consider to improve things like ROI and profits
use_case: If you need a truly robust, full blown accounting system, complete with job-costing and inventory, take a close look at QBE.

use_case: It is amazing. If you have a small business, you need it. Your business is right there at your fingertips. I do not know how a small business can run without it.

likes: I like the flexibility. You can set QBES up to be very simple for ease of use by the client, or it can also be very complex allowing for complex inventory situations and job costing. Plus, there are a ton of add-on applications that expand it's capabilities even further.
dislikes: Probably the think I dislike the most is the fact that it is so easy to modify an existing transaction. The good news is that there is an audit trail, but that adds a whole extra level of documentation that needs reviewed to resolve a problem.
recommendations: We have a ton of clients that use QBES and we also use the software as outsourced accountants and bookkeepers. We are handling every day business transactions and much more, including inventory management and job costing. QuickBooks Desktop Does the Job for 99% of
use_case: If your business deals with more complex situations such as job costing or heavy inventory requirements, QBES is the way to go.

likes: I like the range of options allowed on QB
dislikes: Support center is not always helpful to go to.
use_case: Ensuring customer’s balances are correct, invoicing, bank reconciliations and much more. Very good software for Accounting

likes: Quickbooks lets employers create an account to track sales and manage inventory. It shows the customers names and all payments they've made, and any inventory they've bought.
dislikes: It has a lot of steps when it comes to uploading receipts, but it does allow for that to also help larger businesses specify the location, and accounts.
recommendations: Quickbooks has many tools that can make doing business simple. You can track expenses, and profits and stay extremely organized. You can also download the QuickBooks app and scan and upload the physical receipts as well or simply take a picture of it. Quickbooks For
use_case: Quickbooks will help businesses assess their market and sales. This will allow them to understand their profits and check the graphs to see a rise or drop within each month.

likes: I like how easy it is to pull up reports
dislikes: It is hard to navigate quickbooks on a phone.
use_case: It’s easy to keep track of payroll and year end documents.

likes: We use QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise at my company. It is very simple. We use different entities for US operations and international operations. International operations do accounting in local software and input balances into individual QuickBooks on a monthly basis. When we run consolidation and automatically export into Excel - income statement monthly, year-to-date and balance sheet. The process is very simple and exports data into Excel. This allows us to consolidate 10 entities. I believe that QuickBooks is ideal for small and medium businesses. It is easy to use. There is a lot of training available for QuickBooks. A lot of accountants use QuickBooks, making recruitment easy. Overall, I consider QuickBooks the best system to start your business and use until it grows to a larger corporation.
dislikes: Once business grows, eventually it would outgrowth QuickBooks. QuickBooks is great for medium enterprises, but for large companies, we usually need a larger ERP system. Also, QuickBooks does not allow to keep audit trial. Basically, everyone can go into prior period and change data. As a result, if a company goes public with QuickBooks, it has to retain a lot of paper trial proving review of journal entries and monthly close. This disadvantage is partially offset by ability to "close" period by requiring a password for prior periods. Overall, there are many more advantages than disadvantages of the QuickBooks.
recommendations: We are consolidating 10 entities in QuickBooks, which is huge! Majority of entities are international. QuickBooks exports income statements and balance sheets into Excel, making consolidation process easy. Also, when I came to the company after acquisition, almost whole accounting was gone. It was very easy to find new accountants who use QuickBooks as QuickBooks is very widely used. Additionally, it is very simple and easy to use. Our international accountants required only a minimal training, needed for them to input monthly balances into individual QuickBooks.
use_case: Quickbooks Desktop Enterprise is a perfect accounting system for small and medium businesses. It is very simple to use and set up. It is very widely used. Everyone knows that hiring in accounting and finance is a challenge, due to high demand. It is very easy to find accountants who know QuickBooks Desktop. So, you search is much easier. Also, Quickbooks Desktop Enterprise is a very straighforward and easy to use version. I would say not too much training is required at all, especially, for people who already understand accounting. All reporting is intuitive. Reports are very easy to generate. I very much like that you can just double click on a number and get details of accounting entries. So, it is very easy to see analytics. I really love reporting in QuickBooks. It is so easy and straightforward. I also liked very much "Consolidation" function in QuickBooks that they introduced. You simply run income statements and balance sheets of multiple QuickBooks entities and the function export those together side-to-side in Excel. As a result, you have draft of multiple consolidated entities kept in separate QuickBooks in Excel next to each other and you can do consolidation. It is very easy and handy. I absolutely love it! Also, I noticed some extra very useful reports in QuickBooks Desktop (that are not yet available in QuickBooks online version, although this might be just a timing issue). At my company, we use QuickBooks for 10 consolidated entities with multiple international entities. We loved it! Overall, I would highly recommend QuickBooks.

likes: It's easy to get started with QB and, if you have any experience at all, it's pretty easy to use. That said, the quantity, quality and flexibility of the reporting is probably the best part about QB. You can report on anything in any level of detail, which helps to find both the good and the bad in your business!
dislikes: There are still some basic things that QB doesn't do well, such as the ability to reconcile bank accounts when you use a journal entry with splits against your account. It's a bit nitpicky, but sometimes I have to re-enter transactions to to back out of these types of situations in order to reconcile my accounts. In other words, there are things you have to learn trial-and-error.
recommendations: I'm using QB to both run my own business and keep the books for a Boy Scout troop. Syncing with bank accounts is pretty seamless and tracking some unique needs for Scouting are both pretty easy to do in QB.

likes: Easy to use ... too easy. You can get yourself into trouble without much effort. Does not require actual understanding of accounting principals, which is both good and bad, depending on what you want to be able to do with the product.
dislikes: For me, this is way to simple. The inventory section is lacking, and the entire process takes too long. 10 minutes for a build? We have this hosted with our dealer. Should be onsite / local. The windows for data entry are always changing the column widths. Very frustrating. Remedies are provided but do not work consistently.
recommendations: The only benefit is that it eventually will allow you to get your work done. The GL part of the program and the drill down capabilities are good (mostly), but the bad things out weigh the good things. Would probably be good for a smaller company, although ours is fairly small. We have > 1500 inventory parts, and that bogs down the entire system.
use_case: I don't recommend for manufacturer with more than 500 inventory items, or builds that require sub assemblies, etc.

likes: Somewhat simple to use. Inexpensive, comparatively. It covers the basics well. I think it is definitely intended for a smaller company. It does seem to small for our size company currently. There are some really good elements in place ... i.e. payroll administration
dislikes: Bad database design, locked down (in terms of accessing data). Reports are limited and not easily customized. Not easy to do simple things such as sort by columns, search for customers function is a little weak - it takes a long time. Generating custom reports can take a long time and potentially lock up the application.
recommendations: Basic Accounting. Able to manage finances. Overall customer management. It also houses all of the data for our custom reporting. The basic reporting is pretty helpful as well - although somewhat lacking
use_case: Make sure to read the documentation thoroughly. To ensure that the product is used properly. Customization is allowed but not supported well in the program and database. Would recommend adding something like QQube to enhance the custom reporting capabilities. The reporting within Quickbooks can be rather clunky and difficult to work with. Would also make sure your process flow chart matches QB's flow chart otherwise it will be challenging to customize. Also - there is no way to connect Sales Orders to Purchase orders.

However, challenges came up that the smaller company didn't face due to simpler processes and less overhead.

likes: I like the progressive invoicing option. It keeps more organized and I can track if they have paid only a portion of the invoice or the entire invoice. Also most features are very easy to use. My customers and jobs are easy to have access to and anyone with general knowledge can understand quickly what the situation is with every project. I like that fact that we can personalize our invoice and estimate sheets as well.
dislikes: I dislike the fact that it is somewhat difficult to set up the system for the customer to pay with a credit card easily. I still have not been able to properly set this up. I am not sure if the customer has to give me their credit card number and I save it in the system, or if they should input their information themselves, as they would do on quick books online. Also, also the email logging in that you have to do when sending invoices, after you are already logged on to the system is somewhat irritating. I still do not think that customers get to view the invoice when the open the attachment or click on the link. I always have to send a separate email with the invoice attached for them to view.
recommendations: I would not necessarily say problems, but we are solving, invoicing, payments and expense tracking. Everything is more clear and organized now. Once invoices are created, customers can receive them and pay promptly
use_case: It is definitely a great software to use for invoicing and keeping track of numbers.

likes: I would have to say that the best things about QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise is the easy-to-navigate process menus on top of the familiar user interface (UI) for current QuickBooks users. It has a huge user community not to mention simple forms and report customization. A large number of standard and contributed reports mixed with excellent import capabilities makes this a no brainer.
dislikes: There are no Economic Order Capabilities and it has a basic low inventory warning that doesn't really fit my needs. There is no true central documentation which would make it easier to access and learn on the fly. Plus, the mobile access is an add-on service which is kind of a pain.
recommendations: Business accounting made simple as well as organizing everything for multiple users. It makes it easier when it's a widely recognized system that many people are familiar with.
use_case: QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise is a good intermediate step up for businesses that have outgrown other QuickBook versions. But it has scalability limitations and a proprietary database that might quickly constrain growing midsize companies.

But if you are looking for something that is going to be meatier and more dynamic then keep looking. Quickbooks is great for accounting but lacking a lot in CRM and inventory modules.

likes: The user interface and organization seems to be easy enough for most people to navigate. Great for a single accountant or small business with minimal experience. Simple to use interface and easy to understand reports. Widely used, so easy to find and accountant who can operate the software. Has it ability link into TurboTax for simplified end of year taxes. Part of it Intuit suit of software, so data is more portable between different intuit products.
dislikes: Antiquated networking technology; I would not consider this an enterprise grade application. It works fine as a local file on your hard drive but doesn't seem to have been designed with networking in mind. I've experienced nothing but constant issues with multi-user file access, I do not recommend this software for a multi-user environment. Integrations are not easy to implement, you are better off with more modern cloud based software that has a fully developed and standardized API.
recommendations: It fulfills the need for an accounting software. Helps with sending invoices and retrieving payment from customers. Useful for reports and online banking.
use_case: Carefully evaluate your needs before selecting this software. There are a lot of better alternatives in various cloud based solutions.

recommendations: We are able to have more users logged on at a time. Reporting has improved as well as the search features. Overall just easy to use and keeps our info very organized.
use_case: Much more user friendly than previous Quickbooks versions. Finally allows you to use two monitors. Highly recommended.

Again, seems to be the best accounting software available for very small business (less than 5 million?). The payroll is great as long as you don't need to track a bunch of municipalities. 
We like all the automatic features available. When we switched to a seperate payroll processor, we found the QB side to be so efficient we really don't save any time...just a bit of hassle (and it fixed class tracking for us).

likes: Quickbooks Desktop Enterprise has variety of features that any large business can benefit from, such as custom reports, inventory management, full service payroll and etc
dislikes: About the product itself I like everything. However, when I was utilizing this product I had to use remote desktop access. Quickbooks Desktop Enterprise uses Right Networks for remote desktop service. I think they should offer other options for remote access as well. Right Networks did not always work great with us, was very slow and did not always load correctly. This was one of the reasons why we have actually moved to Quick Books Online.
use_case: All financial reports, end of year tax forms, payroll, paying bills Quickbooks Desktop Enterprise is the Ultimate

likes: I have recently started learning how to use QB and found it quite easy to use and when I had question, I could click the help button that always answered any question. I was able to track sales, cost, do payroll and it is right at my fingertips.
dislikes: There isn't anything I dislike about using QB. I wish I had learned how to use it years ago.
recommendations: I am able to track company growth from past years, know who my best customers are, where I can cut cost and where my best sales are at all times.
use_case: Don't assume that's it hard to learn or not worth the money because this is a wonderful program and worth the time to learn it. It will simplify your life and help you grow you business.

likes: It has the ability to gives us an accounting format for our business
dislikes:
Your slogan "simplify the busness of life" is not true. Intuit has manged to complicate it unnecessarily. First off, customer support is the worst of any large company, and that includes the notorious phone and cable companies that I have ever experienced. I am a small business Enterprise customer and yet no direct lines to its people. Intuit outsources it's customer support to call centers(mostly the Philippines) and while they may be able to speak English, they do not completely understand. I had to repeat my requests and questions several times before I got any semblance of an answer.
No 2- Intuit, by default, puts you on an annual payroll auto-renewal-great for the companies, bad for customers. You cannot change this through Enterprise desktop app nor on-line. Therein lies the problem. It takes multiple calls, transfers, and hours waiting(not an exaggeration) to get to the right department. This is a total waste of time and hugely frustrating.
No 3 Quickbooks keeps increasing the amount charged every year for payroll(I have the desktop enhanced payroll version). Why? Nothing new has been added. I have left three messages to a small business Enterprise representative I worked with last year(yes managed to get a number miraculously).
No 4. Just got off a call and was on the line for 1 hour(and another transfer) and still no resolution to my annual payroll questions, or trying to find out more about Enterprise 18(which we have been considering upgrading from Enterprise 15). By the way, the website for Quickbooks Enterprise is experiencing problems so can’t get info there. This has tested the patience of the most rational reasonable person. We may be going a different direction next year because of such poor communication, contact methods, and customer service.
use_case: See above Complicating the

recommendations: I was bookkeeping for my company's clients. When I started, I had some reconciling to do starting from 10 years prior using the client's bank statements. I realized that Quickbooks was the best way to do this without having to manually reconcile the books.
use_case: Maybe a way to make this easily usable for individuals who need this for personal accounting. For example, with no experience in bookkeeping and reconciling, there is no way I would be able to know how to use this software easily. As with any software, there can also be an interface upgrade to make it more user friendly.

likes: Quickbooks has always been the affordable and reliable way to conduct business. You can choose what plan you want, if you do the online version, starting at $15 a month for the most basic or up to $50 OR you can buy the Desktop Professional for $300 (with only one user). There are also add-ons you can purchase for different prices, such as varying levels of payroll. For a small church like ours we only need the one desktop plan.
dislikes: I like everything about working with Quickbooks. It's a great, user-friendly program to use!
recommendations: We use it to track employee's times and for payroll, mostly. We may start using it for other things later. I am not in charge of decision making or I would definitely be pushing to get it for our company!
use_case: If you are unsure if you want the desktop version, I would recommend doing a monthly trial of the online version which I mentioned earlier.

recommendations: Our company uses Quickbooks for accounts receivable and payables, invoices, payroll, mileage tracking, and so much more. Personally, I jump onto the the software for marketing use by adding customized invoice announcements.

recommendations: I realized that QuickBooks Desktop Enterprise enables all kinds of users. For example, there's the accountant copy that enables external accountants, consultants, or tax preparers to see and modify journal entries made on QuickBooks. bonus_data.title: Great product but there're rooms for improvements

likes: It's fairly easy to pick up the basics, like processing payroll, using the bank register, employee profiles.
dislikes: The reporting is not easy to learn, not enough robust "canned" reports.
use_case: We run payroll, general ledger, AP and AR out of QB. We make it work, certainly not the most powerful system out there.

likes: Importing transactions through the Online Banking feature and easily matching them to QB transactions. Messaging is another great feature. Reports can be exported into Excel fairly easily, and invoices can be sent via Outlook with one click.
dislikes: Software is slow overall, and crashes maybe twice a month. While there is a lot of potential for customization, this takes time and is not very user-friendly from a setup perspective. Lots of potential for other users to edit something incorrectly or erroneously. Notes are disconnected (transaction vs profile), and none of the reports seem to work perfectly on the first try. Additionally, custom reports are quite tricky to create.
recommendations: QuickBooks Desktop allows us to have a complete, multi-user process for purchases. We have a central item list, which is always current. Reconciliations take no time at all, as long as we keep up on our online banking transaction matching.
use_case: While the Desktop version does have some functionality that the Online version does not, I would advise others to weigh the simplicity of Online as a benefit against the missing functionality in Desktop. On one hand, you could potentially rig Desktop to do 95% of everything you want from a bookkeeping software. However, this would take a considerable time investment to set up and to maintain (including education of all users). On the other hand, while Online has some limitations, it seems to be much easier/simplistic. Like a Chromebook vs. a PC, or something. Not to say there's a right or wrong answer, but just to completely consider the work that goes into Desktop, especially since this is more likely the more expensive option.

recommendations: Our company has had a high turnover rate, and training new employees to use QB Desktop Enterprise has been easy due to the accessibility and ease of using the program. The Home window is useful for new users to find tools and features, and multi-user platform and chat features make it simple to train people from remote locations. Quickbooks Desktop

dislikes: I don't like the non-profit iteration of QB Desktop. The terms don't change to best fit for the nonprofit business (like putting donars and grantors in as "Customers"). It also keeps hold of the Profit and Loss statement even though there is no profit in a nonprofit business. It's the small things that upset stakeholders.
recommendations: I've used various version of Quickbooks Desktop throughout my accounting career - spanning over ten years. Each year gives new updates that are well-placed and usually well needed.
use_case: This has the ability to satisfy all the small to medium sized business' needs for accounting. If it's a large business, it might not be the right fit.

likes: This program is extremely user friendly. We have converted all of our business to the QuickBooks platforms so that our owner has continuity across a clothing store, construction company, and a professional laundry service. One office manager can technically run all three businesses.
dislikes: Automatic updates for payroll service need to be double checked to make sure individual withholding or reporting rates are not altered or incorrect when new rates push from QuickBooks.
recommendations: Continuity across multiple businesses, has given the Office Manager for our construction firm the ability to train and update the staff our other businesses even though they are so vastly different! Great software that is easy to learn but still provides all the reporting and income tracking needed for large scale construction and inventory management for a laundry service and commission structures for the clothing store! Enterprise
use_case: Do it! Its user friendly and has great US based support.

switched_from.reason: Just needed to update to include multiple businesses running their own payroll.

likes: Ease of sharing company files, auto report generation. I was able to customize a template for a complicated report that a client needed regularly, which turned the task into something that took mere seconds.
dislikes: Online's inability to open more than two windows. There could have been a lot of work done to improve sharing of info between QBO and QBE

likes: Quickbooks is one of the strong and well-known names in the business for small business accounting software that offers a lot of basic features in the simple package. Pricing is reasonable and there are a lot of user forums out there and training opportunities for free.
dislikes: There are definitely limitations to Quickbooks. For example, if your small business has complex inventory, you might find Quickbooks challenging for tracking movement of your inventory or manufacturing processes. Or if you have a unique costing like FIFO, you might find it challenging to track layers within the system. The audit trail also leaves something to be desired.
use_case: Quickbooks can get you up and running as a small business and provide the majority of tools to meet your needs. Similar to the 80/20 rule, you may run into trouble as you grow as an organization and have more complex business needs to address - like manufacturing or inventory tracking.

bonus_data.title: Quick software to get up and running for small business, but with limitations

likes: I love the easy to use software and it is perfect for small businesses. It is constantly evolving and improving. It makes managing customers and vendors easy. QuickBooks has a lot to offer for the money and comes with an incredible back up service. Lastly, the system has been around for many many years, so it makes it easy to find a bookkeeper with years of experience with the software.
dislikes: The biggest downfall to QuickBooks Enterprise is that it requires a lot of manual oversight and rebuilding especially if your company has a very large inventory on hand. The software allows inventory to go negative causing a lot of internal problems resulting in the company file having to be rebuilt nightly. I also strongly dislike the QuickBooks customer support when you call in. You are transferred to a call center located in another country with a strong language barrier. 9 times out of 10 you have to ask to speak with a supervisor before you can get to a resolution.
recommendations: We have found the reporting in QuickBooks Enterprise to be very beneficial. You can create nearly any report that comes to mind with ease. Whereas, other accounting software systems we have used in the past made it very difficult to run and format certain reports. Adding custom fields to the vendor and customer side also makes reporting a lot easier. QuickBooks Enterprise - Perfect for
use_case: QuickBooks Enterprise is very innovative, easy to use, and equipped for every small business needs. It offers customizable reporting, it is integrated with Excel making exporting easy, and it allows business to track employee information and ensure necessary regulatory compliance. It's a great solution for keeping all financials organized.

switched_from.reason: While Sales Office was very affordable, you get what you pay for. It lacked quality reporting and customization. It also came with very poor customer support. There were certainly more negatives than positives on the accounting side of things.
