use_case: Calucating expenses.

likes:
Can't really say that there is a best feature. However, were to pick one, I'd say that the reports are easy to use, and easy to create. We use QB only partially, mostly for recording of financial data, and for reporting. The invoicing is not done in QB, nor payroll. 
The reports are quite allright, if that is what you only need them for. We mainly create the reports, then export them into Excel for further processing.
dislikes: Given the limited use that we get out of QB, I'd say that having to validate data every time we import something in QB gives a sense of instability of the platform. Data validation sometimes fails without a reason. In order to troubleshoot, you would then need to employ someone with technical skills to follow the logs and troubleshoot. It could get time consuming, and not very productive.
recommendations:
We use QB specifically for accounting, month end close, financial reporting for the board, and financial recording of data (data repository) 
We export the data out of QB, and then we will process it via macros, transform it into custom reports for the C-suite and board directors.
use_case:
Make sure you get the support you need and quickly. Searching online for answers takes time for the uninitiated, and it is unproductive. 
However, if one is tech savvy, reading the file logs will usually get you the troubleshooting answer. The problem is that when QB fails, doesn't give you a reason, mostly says to call Customer Support.

bonus_data.title: QuickBooks does the job, but it has shortcomings

likes: This was simple to learn and use on a daily bases.
dislikes: This is easy to use but it would be nice to be able to upload 1099 so that all financial records could be found in one place.
recommendations: By using this program, I did not have to hire a book keeper to keep up with this information. I also can look t it whenever I need to and understand. I am able to organize the information the way that is best for me to understand an analyze as the business manager.
use_case: Use an alternate program to upload 1099. Make sure you set time aside to understand this program so you can use it to it's full potential.

likes:
Enterprise will serve your accounting needs during the mid-growth phase of a company. The reporting features are robust with the community input and the ability to create and memorize reports. Audit trail is a wonderful feature to track down where an error occurred and allow employee coaching...however there is no search function to the audit trail so be prepared with a pot of coffee when searching for an error - unless you are a small company and you know the exact day the error occurred.

We've been able to rig Enterprise to work with our specific company accounting demands with a little creativity and ingenuity.
dislikes:
A HUGE dislike is the constant, large fees that Intuit is always pushing. When Enterprise stopped being offered via software option and the only option was cloud-based, we were done. No more thousands of dollars to renew & update and no hefty monthly fees. 

Another HUGE dislike is the 1M cap on customers and inventory items with significant software slow-down when a larger list is built.
recommendations: Better control over finances, better reporting features to allow for accurate ROI capture. QuickBooks Enterprise - An Expensive Product for your
use_case:
While I appreciate the cloud-based aspect to QuickBooks now, it is extremely frustrating to pay so much and not own the software. In addition, be sure to be prepared for ALL Intuit expenses relating to Enterprise. There's alway more Intuit seems to want to charge. (ie additional users fee)

If you have a large inventory or a large customer base, be prepared for ridiculously long search times.
reviewer_name: Verified User in Non-Profit Organization Management

switched_from.reason: QuickBooks had a better inventory control system at the time. Cannot compare as we were a much smaller company at the time of the switch.

likes: This is just the standard financial software for small, medium, and large businesses. Quite a few people make addons and APIs for integration with other pieces of software. Also, most of the larger CRMs already contain integrations with Quickbooks which can make it very easy to get a business up and running with some standard pieces of software. Many free CRMs also do this.
dislikes: The backups are a little bit cumbersome with an even moderately large file. I would like to see this process improved so that the time spent doing backups is shorter and can be run automatically at set times throughout the day (or night when users aren't using it).
recommendations: We needed a solution that would integrate with our custom software in order to ease our payroll and billing process. We've connected our custom software using the standard Quickbooks APIs through a 3rd party software to help speed up the process. While this 3rd party software wasn't needed, it has eliminated quite a few lines of code that would have needed to be added. Quickbooks has maintained compatibility with their older APIs throughout the time that we've had Quickbooks, which is very good. The Standard in
use_case: Quickbooks, while taking some work getting the custom pieces setup, is very flexible and can be used for simple setups to very complex setups. There are lower grades of QuickBooks for smaller businesses to use, but Quickbooks Enterprise has all the features for medium and larger businesses.

There's also no great way to create bundled items in Quickbooks that will work with our other integrated softwares.

likes: Very flexible for the particulars common to small business accounting needs. Larger companies that require accounting departments should look at the competition's offering. I've never used QB outside of a small company so that's my context.
dislikes: Very easy for users to make changes they really shouldn't be making. Can be somewhat avoided with permissions. Would be nice to have cleaner data exports for use with the 3rd party apps. We've found that QB doesn't inherently offer great reports for our needs. Would be nice to have better automated cost allocations for overhead.
recommendations: Has given us improved cost accounting for our construction jobs. Previously, we relied just on Excel to determine if each job was profitable but now we do so with QB.
use_case: Good to use other accounting packages just to know what you've got with QB. It's flexibility is, to me, both its greatest strength and weakness. Those with ill intentions could easily get away with data deletion to cover their tracks; have gone through that before, not good for anyone. But, the ability to change entries easily for legitimate reasons is valuable to small businesses lacking a department full of accountants. Not the most robust in regards to reporting but there are many 3rd party apps that can use the exported data.

bonus_data.title: We started detailing our costs more recently and it has been much easier to find odd charges that otherwise could get lost in the shuffle. Manager

likes: What I like best is the ability to keep all account information in one program. I also like how easy it is to use, it seems intuitive the way it works!
dislikes: Even though it is easy to use, there is a learning curve and I found that there wasn't much information beyond obvious operation. Once you did something wrong, lost information, or things didn't work like they should I was at a loss as to what to do. I like to click around and solve my own issues.
recommendations: The problems I'm solving is the overlapping of jobs done by several people are all done in the same platform so you know what has been entered, printed, invoiced, and eliminate duplication of work.
use_case: There is a myriad of information out there so be sure it covers all the bases you require! I find it is intuitive to use and works for all our accounting issues, from inventory (ordering and receiving) to check writing and everything in between.

I like the Flexible options within the application. We've been able to set it up to provide the functionality we require to accurately input and track orders and sales. It generates important reports, allows controlled access as well as multiple users. We can also access multiple companies, handy for tracking inter-company transactions.

Finally, it will grow with us. We don't see a near term need to move to a more expensive platform.

recommendations: It helps us track inventory, invoicing and purchases.

dislikes: I am not a big fan having to worry about getting kicked off the program, due to the "maxed out number of licenses" being used during the day. Literally only 4-5 people can be on the program at once, especially when we all need it.

use_case: I would recommend Quickbooks if you are a company that is looking for a program that makes everything simple and easy to understand; especially if you are needing to get to PO's, Invoices and Vendor information quickly.
reviewer_name: Verified User in Health, Wellness and Fitness

likes: I was a big fan of the smart search feature. This feature made finding items and asking questions much easier. The report filters were another feature that made my experience much better and easier. I was able to hone in on the data that was most relevant to the task at hand. One of the most important things about Quickbooks is its reliability and security. In today's world there is a lot of cyber attacks and with so much important data being put into Quickbooks, I am glad to know they have very tight security protocols.
dislikes: I sometimes run into performance issues. For example I will sometimes click on a file and it will take a decent amount of time to finally open up. It is almost as if there is some sort of lag with the program. This is more prevalent when I first start using the program as opposed to later on in the day.
recommendations: This program helps cut down wasted time for myself and my employees. The program basically does so much of the small and simple bookkeeping tasks that normally take up precious time throughout the day. The filters alone in Qucikbooks drastically cut down on the amount of time employees need to complete their bookkeeping tasks.
use_case: Quickbooks is an absolute must! If you are a small business owner it is imperative that you purchase Quickbooks. It will save you time and money in the long run.

dislikes: Not much, but more discounted trainings would be nice. Also, I wish Quicken for personal use was more like Quickbooks.
recommendations: Reconcilations and our annual audit. That everything is very traceable and user friendly.
use_case: none at this time!

likes: Compared to the Desktop and Desktop Premier versions, QB Enterprise reporting functionality and capabilities are vastly superior. QB Enterprise allows easy reporting by employee in a manner that was previously very difficult. This is critical in a not-for-profit environment because of the reporting requirements to allocate expenses between program, management, and fundraising expenses in order to prepare the statement of functional expenses.
dislikes: I disliked that the software does not automatically account for the revenues and expenses from fundraising events or auctions correctly. Instead, the user of the software must make manual journal entries to account for the special not-for-profit accounting treatment for these types of events.
recommendations: QuickBooks Enterprise is an excellent solution for not-for-profits with multiple donor restricted revenues and assets that require careful accounting. The platform is able to scale up to probably around the $10M annual revenue range before a more robust software solution would be necessary. I found that although QB Enterprise was not necessarily designed with not-for-profits in mind it certainly is very useful compared to the previous versions of QuickBooks or even software that is designed specifically for small not-for-profit organizations. QB Enterprise -
use_case: Take the time to set up your accounts right the first time. In fact, pay an external consultant to help with this process. The value of this is many times the initial cash outlay.

use_case: If you are going to use it as a basic accounting program - you are in luck! Quickbooks is easy to use, easy to learn and easy to implement.

likes: I like QuickBooks Enterprise's robust functionality compared to other inventory programs on the market for small businesses. It's really not possible to run a manufacturing business with any of the other lower-tier options.
dislikes: There are more things to dislike them to like about QuickBooks. It's processes are rigid and often don't match with actual business practices and a small start up. For example, an invoice removes inventory, and there is no other tool and QuickBooks to collect payment from the customer. If you require customer payment prior to inventory removal (order fulfillment), then workflows become overly complicated. Additionally, this strange inventory/invoice combination doesn't allow you to separate accounts receivable from warehousing/fulfillment. Similar problems are present in the rigid PO/Item Receipt/Bill process.
recommendations: We are solving our need for daily account management, fulfillment management, and bookkeeping. There are clear benefits to having all of this in one program, but the benefits are tempered by the difficulty of use.
use_case: If you're considering QuickBooks enterprise, know that switching from another version of QuickBooks, particularly the online version, is not as easy as they advertise. Since different QuickBooks versions have different functionalities, not everything matches up as it should. After 6 months, we're still sorting through the errors.

recommendations: We are solving a problem with an unbalanced use of work-hours. likes: For a person not particularly well versed in accounting the software is fairly straight forward. The software well organizes the information you give in a user-friendly fashion.
dislikes:
For two years I worked in an engineering company's accounting office where QuickBooks was used to maintain records. As an accounting staff I was responsible for producing reports, making journal entries, entering new clients, updating records, and occasionally producing financial statements. My experience is this: for a person not particularly well versed in accounting the software is fairly straight forward (as stated above). However, for an accountant the software limits the ability to customize. 
As well in my experience, there were 1 too many times the software had glitches and we had to contact the customer service line to get assistance and correct a problem. Specifically, there was 1 day where I got into work and didn't have access to the software for 4 hours which is a major inefficiency in an accounting department.
recommendations:
I am reading this as "what did you use the software for, and did it help you?"

In my use, the business used QuickBooks to solve the issue of organizing clientele records, invoice/accounts payable records, projects, employee time cards, reports. The benefits were many - the software did what we needed it to do. In retrospect, I wish it did more than just that. But the software does exactly what it says it will do for your business. likes: Security access rights provide flexibility for a company who needs more than one person entering data. With nearly 115 different toggles, the administrator can develop a "Role" by assigning access to specific areas and the level of access to that area. A Shipping and Receiving Role may only have access to Purchase Orders, an Accounts Receivable Role may have access to the Customer Center, the Accounts Receivable account and Accounts Receivable reports, but no access to other areas. It truly takes the load off of just one data entry person.
dislikes: QuickBooks Enterprise has always been marketed toward the midsize business. Unfortunately, may Switchers (those coming off of a more complex software) are not always happy with the level of sophistication in the features offered. QuickBooks Enterprise has ramped up a number of items, such as Advanced Inventory and Advanced Pricing, but manufacturers are still going elsewhere for job shop processes and LIFO costing.
recommendations: Currently, I am working on a process flow for a company who sources bedroom products. There are work arounds that we are handling together, including tracking a Customer PO through the whole process, Ship To in the Purchase Order cannot have multiple addresses, adding several custom fields in order to automate information appearing on POs and Invoices and customized templates. 5 Reasons Why QuickBooks Enterprise is Right for
use_case: Half the battle is understanding your business flow of information and your reporting requirements. Make sure you qualify the product as one that will accommodate your needs.
